title: lomatium utriculatum
intro: Lomatium utriculatum, also known as Indian basket grass or Indian licorice root, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America and has been traditionally used by indigenous peoples for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Lomatium utriculatum can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried root, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
